steps.title: Spot the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content: The shell's external appearance is the reason people mistakenly assume it's an exoskeleton—the misconception directly stems from what the shell looks like.

steps.title: Apply the causal transition
steps.content:
”Hence” means “therefore”—it connects the external appearance to the resulting misconception, showing that people's mistaken assumption flows directly from what the shell looks like. “However” introduces contradiction; “That being said” signals a separate point; “For instance” suggests an example—none of these capture the causal relationship here.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
